catalog.title: Listen & read · Ears and eyes on realistic turns
catalog.description: Ears and eyes on realistic turns · A2 listen & read in “Admin & services”: Dutch you can use the same week, guided steps, and a short check at the end.

pedagogy.objective: By the end of this lesson, you can understand short input about admin & services, notice useful chunks, and answer gist/detail questions
can_do_outcomes: Understand basic instructions at a service desk (nummer trekken, wachten)
Read short official letters or emails with familiar vocabulary
Follow a short exchange in this theme and reuse one chunk.

grammar_points.point: conditional polite forms (Ik zou graag … willen)
examples_nl: Zou u dit formulier kunnen controleren?
Ik zou graag een afspraak willen voor een ID-kaart.
examples_en: I'd like an appointment for an ID card.

examples_nl: De pasfoto die ik meeneem, is recent.
Het document dat u nodig heeft, staat online.
examples_en: The passport photo I'm bringing is recent.
The document you need is online.

examples_nl: Zonder DigiD loop ik naar het loket.
Ik moet mijn adres bij de gemeente wijzigen.
examples_en: Without DigiD I go to the desk.
I have to change my address at the municipality.

micro_outcomes: I can recognise key words about admin & services in short Dutch texts or audio.
I can copy at least one useful chunk into a sentence about myself.
I can finish most guided tasks in the lesson without peeking at translations on the second try.
I can pass the quick self-check at the end of the lesson.
I know what to revise tomorrow in two minutes or less.

steps.activity:
Welcome! This lesson is built in small steps so you always know where you are.

**What you'll be able to do:** Understand short input about admin & services, notice useful chunks, and answer gist/detail questions

**Grammar thread today:** Short relative clauses (die / dat) — you'll get explanations, examples, and production tasks in **Language focus** and **Guided practice**.

Take your time — you can repeat any step. Try to say at least one Dutch sentence out loud today.
steps.teacher_notes: Keep under ~1 min; reassure anxious learners.

steps.activity:
Let's wake up vocabulary about **Admin & services**.

Words you might use today:
• **overschrijving**
• **postpakket**
• **handtekening**
• **gemeente**
• **paspoort**
• **verblijfsvergunning**
• **DigiD**
• **afspraak**

**Your turn (1–2 short sentences, Dutch or mixed):**
**Wat deed je gisteren?** — *What did you do yesterday?*

If you only know a few words, that's fine — start there.
steps.example_response: Gisteren ging ik naar de **gemeente** voor een afspraak. Daarna deed ik een kopie van een document en postte ik een brief.

steps.activity:
**Listen & read** this short conversation. Read twice: first for gist, then for details.

**Postmedewerker:** Waar gaat dit pakket naartoe?

**Klant:** Naar Spanje. Hoeveel kost verzending?

**Postmedewerker:** Met track & trace is het veertien euro. Zonder trace goedkoper, maar langzamer.

**Klant:** Dan neem ik trace. Het is een cadeau.

**Gist check:** Who are the people, and what is the situation?
**Detail check:** Note one time phrase or place you recognise.

**Today's grammar — *die* / *dat* (short relatives)**

**Rule:** *Het formulier **dat** u nodig heeft* — *de pasfoto **die** u meeneemt*.

**Patterns:**
• *Het document dat online staat.* · *De bon die ik zoek.*

**Produce:**
1. Combine two short ideas with one *die/dat* clause.
2. Read aloud slowly — stress the noun, not every function word.

**Pronunciation (listen + repeat)**
**Pronunciation — v / f:** *vandaag, vier* — light *v*, not English *w*.

**Language focus — patterns you can reuse**

**conditional polite forms (Ik zou graag … willen)**
• NL: Zou u dit formulier kunnen controleren? / Ik zou graag een afspraak willen voor een ID-kaart.
• EN: Could you check this form? / I'd like an appointment for an ID card.

**passive-like bureaucratic phrases (wordt verwerkt) — recognition**
• NL: De pasfoto die ik meeneem, is recent. / Het document dat u nodig heeft, staat online.
• EN: The passport photo I'm bringing is recent. / The document you need is online.

**relative clauses with die/dat (very short)**
• NL: Zonder DigiD loop ik naar het loket. / Ik moet mijn adres bij de gemeente wijzigen.
• EN: Without DigiD I go to the desk. / I have to change my address at the municipality.

**Try this:** pick one Dutch sentence above and change only the time or the person (ik → wij / vandaag → morgen), **or** do the “Produce” task from the grammar box above.

**1 — Match** (say the pairs out loud)
• **overschrijving** ↔ a word you connect with daily life
• **postpakket** ↔ a place or thing from this unit

**2 — Gap-fill**
Complete: Ik _____ om acht uur. *(opstaan → stem: Ik **sta** … **op**)*

**3 — Mini dialogue**
**A:** Hoe laat ben je klaar? **B:** Om half zes, denk ik.

**4 — Find the odd one**
Which word fits *least* with this unit: **handtekening**, **broodje**, **trein**? Why?

**5 — True / false**
Say **waar** or **niet waar**: "In Dutch, the verb often moves when you ask a yes/no question."

**6 — Your sentence**
Write one true sentence about yourself using **overschrijving** or **postpakket**.

**7 — Transform**
Make this informal line a bit more formal: "Kun je me helpen?" → start with **Kunt u …**

**8 — Quick translation check**
"Zou u dit formulier kunnen controleren?" — what does it mean in English (your own words)?
---

**All four skills — finish this lesson with these four micro-tasks**

🎧 **Listening:** Use TTS on this Dutch line (slow, then closer to natural): *Postmedewerker: Waar gaat dit pakket naartoe?*

📖 **Reading:** Read silently, then say the **main idea in one English sentence**: *Postmedewerker: Waar gaat dit pakket naartoe?*

✍️ **Writing:** Write **one new Dutch sentence** that uses **overschrijving** and fits *Short relative clauses (die / dat)*.

🗣️ **Speaking:** Read your new sentence **aloud twice** — exaggerate stress the first time, normal the second.

*(If this lesson was already heavy on one skill, treat that skill as “extra reps” — the point is you always touch all four.)*
steps.teacher_notes: Mix modalities; at least 6 tasks — all concrete above.

steps.activity:
**Phrase bank (use freely)**
• overschrijving
• postpakket
• handtekening
• gemeente
• paspoort
• verblijfsvergunning
• DigiD
• afspraak
• loket
• wachtrij
• bankpas
• rekening

**Your task:** Write or say **4–6 short sentences** in Dutch about this unit theme.
Rules: include **at least one question** and one connector (**en**, **maar**, or **want**).

**Example starter (you can change it):**
Vandaag … Ik wil … **want** … **En jij?**

**Quick self-check**

Try each question, then tap **Check answer**. The correct answer stays hidden until you submit.

**Tip:** Say Dutch options out loud if that helps.

**Afterwards:** If three or more felt easy, open **Quiz** for a short recap. If not, go back to **Guided practice** once more.
steps.teacher_notes: Rendered with interactive UI when interaction is present.

items.prompt: Pick the best reply when someone says **Hoe gaat het?**
items.feedbackCorrect: Nice — this is a natural, friendly reply.
items.feedbackIncorrect: **Tot ziens** is goodbye; **Graag gedaan** fits after *dank je*. After *Hoe gaat het?* people often say how they feel, e.g. **Goed, dank je!**

items.hint: Present tense of *leren* (to learn), 1st person singular.

items.prompt: Which opening sounds **more formal** when you talk to a stranger or in a shop?
items.feedbackCorrect: Right — **u** + verb form (*Kunt u…*) is the usual polite pattern.
items.feedbackIncorrect: **Kunt u** with *u* is more formal; **Kun je** is fine with friends.

items.prompt: In Dutch, yes/no questions often put the **verb before the subject** (e.g. *Ga je mee?*).
items.feedbackCorrect: Correct — verb-first order is typical for yes/no questions.
items.feedbackIncorrect: This is **true**: many yes/no questions start with the verb.

items.prompt: Say or write **one new sentence** using **overschrijving**.
items.exampleAnswer: Example: *Ik wil graag overschrijving met mijn nieuwe buren.* (adjust to your situation.)

steps.activity:
**Nice work.**

Next unit starts with **Listen & read · Conversation routines you can reuse**. You can preview the new words in Browse whenever you like.

**2-minute revision:** skim the phrase bank from step 6 once more.

When you're ready, open **Quiz** for a compact recap — or **Flashcards** for the lemmas.

**Dutch culture insight**

PostNL and **track-and-trace** texts are everyday reading practice. *Niet thuis?* often means a neighbour or pickup point (*afhaalpunt*).

**Phrases — gemeente desk**

| Dutch | English |
|-------|--------|
| *Ik zou graag een afspraak willen.* | I'd like an appointment. |
| *Welke documenten heb ik nodig?* | Which documents do I need? |
| *Kan ik dit online met DigiD?* | Can I do this online with DigiD? |

**Mini dialogue**

**Balie:** Goedemorgen, waarmee kan ik u helpen?
**Inwoner:** Ik wil me inschrijven op dit adres. Wat moet ik laten zien?
**Balie:** Paspoort, huurcontract en een bankafschrift van de afgelopen maand.

**Say or write:** Ask what you need to bring for one appointment.

*Optional reflection:* In one English sentence, what feels similar or different where you live?
steps.teacher_notes: Link to next lesson id in CMS when wired.

quiz_ideas: Self-check: choose the best reply to "Hoe gaat het?" (see step 7 in the lesson).
Gap-fill idea: write a sentence with **overschrijving** in the present tense.
True/false: in Dutch yes/no questions the verb often comes before the subject.
Say one new polite request starting with **Kunt u …** or **Mag ik …**.
Paraphrase one Dutch line from the lesson input in your own English.
assessment.success_criteria: You can complete the step-7 self-check comfortably and produce at least one clear Dutch sentence about the lesson theme (spoken or written).

content_refs.notes: Slow audio assumed for lines marked *slow* in builder CMS.
